foreground.h(3) Library Functions Manual foreground.h(3)

NAME

foreground.h

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * image_print (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_256color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t *image, bool use_background, const char *palette)

Function Documentation

char * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)

Definition at line 27 of file foreground.c.

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[luma_idx];
102 
103       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
104       int j = x + 1;
105       while (j < w) {
106         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
107         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
108         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
109         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
110         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
111         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
112           break;
113         j++;
114       }
115       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
116 
117       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
118       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
119       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
120         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
121       } else {
122         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
123           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
124         }
125       }
126       x = j;
127     }
128 
129     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
130     if (y != h - 1) {
131       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
132     }
133   }
134 
135   ob_term(&ob);
136   return ob.buf;
137 }








char
  * image_print_16color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 518 of file foreground.c.


518                                                                      {
519   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
520     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
521     return NULL;
522   }
523 
524   int h = image->h;
525   int w = image->w;
526 
527   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
528     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
529     return NULL;
530   }
531 
532   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
533   ansi_fast_init_16color();
534 
535   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
536   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
537   // Calculate with overflow checking
538   size_t h_times_w;
539   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
540     return NULL;
541   }
542 
543   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
544   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
545     return NULL;
546   }
547 
548   size_t buffer_size;
549   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
550     return NULL;
551   }
552 
553   char *buffer;
554   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
555 
556   char *ptr = buffer;
557   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
558 
559   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
560   if (!utf8_cache) {
561     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color rendering");
562     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
563     return NULL;
564   }
565 
566   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
567     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
568       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
569 
570       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
571       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
572       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
573 
574       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
575       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
576 
577       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
578       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
579       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
580       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
581 
582       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
583       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
584       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
585 
586       if (char_info) {
587         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
588         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
589           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
590         }
591       } else {
592         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
593         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
594         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
595         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
596       }
597     }
598 
599     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
600     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
601     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
602     if (y < h - 1) {
603       *ptr++ = '\n';
604     }
605   }
606 
607   *ptr = '\0';
608   return buffer;
609 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 635 of file foreground.c.


635                                                                               {
636   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
637     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
638     return NULL;
639   }
640 
641   int h = image->h;
642   int w = image->w;
643 
644   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
645     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
646     return NULL;
647   }
648 
649   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
650   ansi_fast_init_16color();
651 
652   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
653   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
654   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
655   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
656 
657   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
658   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
659   // Calculate with overflow checking
660   size_t h_times_w2;
661   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w2) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
662     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
663     return NULL;
664   }
665 
666   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;
667   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w2, 12u, &h_times_w2_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
668     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
669     return NULL;
670   }
671 
672   size_t buffer_size;
673   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
674     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
675     return NULL;
676   }
677 
678   char *buffer;
679   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
680 
681   char *ptr = buffer;
682   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
683 
684   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
685   if (!utf8_cache) {
686     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered rendering");
687     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
688     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
689     return NULL;
690   }
691 
692   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
693     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
694       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
695 
696       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
697       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
698       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
699 
700       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
701       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
702 
703       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
704       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
705       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
706       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
707 
708       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
709       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
710       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
711 
712       if (char_info) {
713         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
714         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
715           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
716         }
717       } else {
718         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
719         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
720         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
721         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
722       }
723     }
724 
725     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
726     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
727     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
728     if (y < h - 1) {
729       *ptr++ = '\n';
730     }
731   }
732 
733   *ptr = '\0';
734   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
735   return buffer;
736 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


Definition at line 739 of file foreground.c.


739                                                                                                                    {
740   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
741     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
742     return NULL;
743   }
744 
745   int h = image->h;
746   int w = image->w;
747 
748   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
750     return NULL;
751   }
752 
753   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
754   ansi_fast_init_16color();
755 
756   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
757   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
758   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
759   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
760 
761   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
762   size_t buffer_size =
763       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
764   char *buffer;
765   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
766 
767   char *ptr = buffer;
768   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
769 
770   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
771   if (!utf8_cache) {
772     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered bg rendering");
773     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
774     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
775     return NULL;
776   }
777 
778   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
779     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
780       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
781 
782       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
783       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
784 
785       if (use_background) {
786         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
787         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
788         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
789 
790         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
791         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
792         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
793 
794         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
795         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
796       } else {
797         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
798         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
799       }
800 
801       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
802       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
803 
804       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
805       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
806       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
807       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
808 
809       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
810       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
811       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
812 
813       if (char_info) {
814         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
815         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
816           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
817         }
818       } else {
819         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
820         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
821         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
822         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
823       }
824     }
825 
826     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
827     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
828     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
829     if (y < h - 1) {
830       *ptr++ = '\n';
831     }
832   }
833 
834   *ptr = '\0';
835   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
836   return buffer;
837 }










char
  * image_print_256color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 413 of file foreground.c.


413                                                                       {
414   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
415     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
416     return NULL;
417   }
418 
419   int h = image->h;
420   int w = image->w;
421 
422   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_256color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   ansi_fast_init_256color();
428 
429   // Buffer size: 256-color ANSI codes are "\033[38;5;NNNm" (max 13 bytes) + char + reset + newline
430   size_t h_times_w;
431   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
432     return NULL;
433   }
434 
435   size_t h_times_w_times_18;
436   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 18u, &h_times_w_times_18) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
437     return NULL;
438   }
439 
440   size_t buffer_size;
441   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_18, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
442     return NULL;
443   }
444 
445   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
446   char *ptr = buffer;
447   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
448 
449   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
450   if (!utf8_cache) {
451     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 256-color rendering");
452     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
453     return NULL;
454   }
455 
456   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
457     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
458       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
459 
460       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
461       ptr = append_256color_fg(ptr, color_index);
462 
463       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
464 
465       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
466       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
467       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
468 
469       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
470       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
471 
472       if (char_info) {
473         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
474           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
475         }
476       } else {
477         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
478         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
479         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
480       }
481     }
482 
483     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
484     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
485     if (y < h - 1) {
486       *ptr++ = '\n';
487     }
488   }
489 
490   *ptr = '\0';
491   return buffer;
492 }










char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 191 of file foreground.c.


191                                                                {
192   if (!p || !palette) {
193     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
194     return NULL;
195   }
196   if (!p->pixels) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
198     return NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
202   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
203   if (!utf8_cache) {
204     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
205     return NULL;
206   }
207 
208   const int h = p->h;
209   const int w = p->w;
210 
211   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
212   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
213   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
214   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
215 
216   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
217   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
218 
219   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
220   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
221     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
222     return NULL;
223   }
224 
225   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
226   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
227 
228   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
229   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
230     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
231     return NULL;
232   }
233 
234   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
235 
236   // Per row: newline (except last row)
237   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
238   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
239   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
240 
241   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
242   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
243 
244   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
245     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
246     return NULL;
247   }
248 
249   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
250   char *lines;
251   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
252 
253   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
254   // char *current_pos = lines;
255   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
256 
257   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
258   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
259   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
260   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
261   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
262 
263   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
264   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
265     const int row_offset = y * w;
266 
267     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
268       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
269       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
270       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation
271       const int luminance = (77 * r + 150 * g + 29 * b + 128) >> 8;
272 
273       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
274       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
275       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
276 
277       // For RLE, we need to pass the first byte of the UTF-8 character
278       // Note: RLE system may need updates for full UTF-8 support
279       const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
280 
281       ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
282     }
283 
284     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
285     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
286       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
287     }
288   }
289 
290   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
291 
292   // DEBUG: Validate frame integrity right after rendering
293   // Check for incomplete ANSI sequences line by line
294   {
295     const char *line_start = lines;
296     int line_num = 0;
297     while (*line_start) {
298       const char *line_end = line_start;
299       while (*line_end && *line_end != '\n')
300         line_end++;
301 
302       // Check for incomplete ANSI sequence at end of this line
303       for (const char *p = line_start; p < line_end;) {
304         if (*p == '\033' && p + 1 < line_end && *(p + 1) == '[') {
305           const char *seq_start = p;
306           p += 2;
307           // Scan for terminator (@ through ~, i.e., 0x40-0x7E)
308           while (p < line_end && !(*p >= '@' && *p <= '~'))
309             p++;
310           if (p >= line_end) {
311             // Incomplete sequence found
312             size_t incomplete_len = (size_t)(line_end - seq_start);
313             log_error("RENDER_INCOMPLETE_ANSI: Line %d (w=%d h=%d) has incomplete ANSI sequence (%zu bytes)", line_num,
314                       w, h, incomplete_len);
315             // Show the incomplete sequence
316             char debug_buf[128] = {0};
317             size_t debug_pos = 0;
318             for (const char *d = seq_start; d < line_end && debug_pos < sizeof(debug_buf) - 10; d++) {
319               unsigned char c = (unsigned char)*d;
320               if (c == '\033') {
321                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = '<';
322                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'E';
323                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'S';
324                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'C';
325                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = '>';
326               } else if (c >= 0x20 && c < 0x7F) {
327                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = (char)c;
328               } else {
329                 debug_pos += (size_t)safe_snprintf(debug_buf + debug_pos, sizeof(debug_buf) - debug_pos, "<%02X>", c);
330               }
331             }
332             log_error("  Incomplete seq: %s", debug_buf);
333             break;
334           } else {
335             p++; // Skip terminator
336           }
337         } else {
338           p++;
339         }
340       }
341 
342       line_num++;
343       line_start = *line_end ? line_end + 1 : line_end;
344     }
345   }
346 
347   return lines;
348 }












Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
